reasoning.name: Reasoning & Decision-Making
reasoning.description: Boros forms decisions through structured reasoning processes that transform available information (inputs, memory, constraints) into coherent, goal-directed actions. Reasoning operates independently of external retrieval and focuses on how decisions are derived.
reasoning.final_state: An agent that consistently decomposes problems, evaluates multiple possible actions, selects optimal paths based on constraints and expected utility, and adapts its reasoning strategy based on outcomes.

reasoning_types.deductive: Applies rules and constraints to derive guaranteed conclusions
reasoning_types.abductive: Selects the most plausible explanation under uncertainty
reasoning_types.evaluative: Ranks candidate actions based on expected utility

decision_policy.goal_alignment: Actions must directly contribute to the objective
decision_policy.constraint_satisfaction: Respect all system and task constraints

rubric.level_1: No structured reasoning. Reactive outputs with no evaluation.
rubric.level_2: Partial reasoning. Limited decomposition and weak evaluation.
rubric.level_3: Structured reasoning with multiple candidates and consistent evaluation.
rubric.level_4: Fully systematic reasoning. Every decision is derived from explicit multi-step evaluation and continuously improved.

independence_from.web_search: Reasoning determines how decisions are made. Web search only supplies additional information when needed.
independence_from.memory_continuity: Reasoning uses memory but does not guarantee persistence of knowledge.

web_search.name: Web Search & Knowledge Retrieval
web_search.description: Boros retrieves external information from the web to supplement internal knowledge. Web search focuses on identifying knowledge gaps, acquiring relevant information, and validating source quality before use.
web_search.final_state: An agent that reliably detects when external knowledge is required, retrieves high-quality information efficiently, validates it across sources, and provides structured inputs for reasoning and decision-making.

search_triggering.knowledge_gap_detection: Triggers when required information is missing
search_triggering.novelty_detection: Triggers for unfamiliar or dynamic domains

query_generation.multi_query_strategy: Uses multiple query variations to improve coverage

output_contract.structured_output: Return distilled, relevant facts—not raw dumps
output_contract.source_traceability: Maintain links between claims and sources

rubric.level_1: No effective search. Relies only on internal knowledge.
rubric.level_2: Basic search with weak queries and poor validation.
rubric.level_3: Structured search with reasonable validation and refinement.
rubric.level_4: Highly systematic retrieval. Precise queries, strong validation, and reliable knowledge extraction.

independence_from.reasoning: Web search retrieves information. Reasoning determines how that information is used.
independence_from.memory_continuity: Web search provides new knowledge, but memory determines persistence.
